candidates.stmt:
Putting Suffolk's Priorities First
I understand our county’s real challenges – long NHS waiting times, social care pressures, poor roads, and school needs – because I’ve spent my career tackling them head-on for local people.

A Proven Record of Responsibility
With two decades improving NHS and public services, I’ve delivered patient-friendly innovations, and achieved real results. A Chartered Manager with a First-Class Honours degree.

Standing Up for Our Community
As a volunteer Freedom to Speak Up Ambassador and former chair of an NHS staff disability network, I champion fairness, inclusion, and the courage to speak out for better services.

A Personal Commitment
I’ll bring my experience, energy, and deep local knowledge to fight tirelessly for Abbeygate & Minden on Suffolk County Council and Abbeygate on West Suffolk District Council.

Paul Hopfensperger (aka "Hoffy") is and has always been devoted to the people who elect him. He believes that’s what being a Politician is all about. Having served his apprenticeship as a Vice Chairman of a political party and becoming Bury St Edmunds Town Councillor for Eastgate Ward in 2003 then Suffolk County Councillor for Tower Division in 2005, he decided to become an Independent Councillor (i.e. not affiliated to any political party) after being told by the party to vote to close down Middle Schools on his Division "or else", despite 18,000 people asking him not to. That was not Paul's idea of representing the people who elected him!

Paul is currently an Independent Bury St Edmunds Town Councillor (Deputy Mayor of Bury St Edmunds) and West Suffolk Councillor for St Olaves Ward in Bury St Edmunds, an area he has served as a Councillor since 2005. Paul is standing as an Independent Parliamentary Candidate for Bury St Edmunds at the next General Election on December 12, 2019.

It’s Paul's intense dedication, passion, and devotion to represent the people, that fuels Paul's fight for change. Explore his site to learn more about the issues that Paul is concerned with and his plans to tackle the problems faced by his constituents on a daily basis.

Tom has a career in business. Having done stints working and living abroad, Tom is based in Suffolk, where his heart is, and lives in Framlingham with his wife and with their two children. If he is successful in the May election, Tom will offer up his time and energy to serve the local community, and particularly wishes to drive improvements to the leisure and sports facilities available for the local community.

Brad is a lifelong Kesgrave resident, with the exception of four years away at the University of Kent where he earned a BA and an MA in History. Brad first joined the Liberal Democrats in 2016, feeling increasingly fed up with the direction the Conservatives were taking the country following the 2015 general election and 2016 EU referendum.

Since returning to Kesgrave from University in 2018, Brad has been Youth Representative for the East Suffolk Liberal Democrats, and is a passionate believer in addressing the issues faced by young people, especially the lack of affordable housing and rising youth unemployment. He is also a strong believer in addressing the inadequate transport in Kesgrave, wanting to see improved provision of alternate transport to cars both for travel within the community and beyond.

t has been an honour to represent Thingoe
North since 2005. Following boundary
changes, I am standing for Barrow and
Thingoe. With experience on the County
Council Cabinet since 2013 and as a district
councillor since 2011, I have a proven track
record of delivering for our communities.
With deep roots in Bury St Edmunds, I live
locally with my husband and our two children
and run a small business giving me real
insight into the pressures facing families and
businesses, and the drive to stand up for
local people.
I’m proud to have a strong record of action
improving road safety, supporting local
businesses, and protecting our countryside.
This election is about delivery, not empty
promises, and I will always be a strong voice
for our community.
This election is a clear choice: continue with a
proven plan that delivers, or risk going
backwards. We’ve made real progress, but
there’s more to do and with your support, we
can keep moving forward and build a
stronger future for Barrow and Thingoe

I have lived in Barrow for 43 yrs . I worked in the  Brewery and property businesses from 1968 starting at Greene King & Sons plc in 1983. I was voted in as a St Edmundsbury Borough Council in 2003, voted in as Mayor in 2010 continued as District Councillor for West Suffolk in 2019 to date, joining Reform UK in January 2024.
Within the Councils I have held the Chair of Audit & Scrutiny, Financial Resilience. Also been a Member of Development Control since 2003 and was Cabinet Member for Finance and Resources.
I am a family man with four children , now with eight grandchildren, all of whom were raised and schooled in the area.
Barrow & Thingoe is a predominantly rural area, I am therefore keen that 
1 Our roads and lanes are fit for purpose for residents, businesses and farmers alike
2 Villages need good community centres  and will assist to have these maintained and modernised.
3 Resolving to ensure that when development happen they respect the historical settings of the villages and these villages do not coalesce with nearbt towns

I am fully committed to representing and serving the people of Barrow and Thingoe with energy, visibility, and dedication.

Barrow and Thingoe is a place people are proud to call home. From villages to growing communities, we need investment that keeps pace with change protecting what matters while planning for the future. Too often, residents feel decisions are made without them. I want to change that.

As your councillor, I will focus on the issues that matter most to local people:

* Supporting families through the cost-of-living crisis by pushing for fairer funding and protecting essential services.

*Improving roads, transport, and infrastructure, so people can travel safely and reliably.

*Championing education and young people, ensuring every child gets the support and opportunities they deserve.

*Protecting vital local services, including social care, libraries, and community spaces.

*Giving residents a stronger voice, making sure your concerns are heard and acted upon.

I bring experience of working with communities, building partnerships, and getting things done. I know how important it is to listen, to be visible, and to follow through on commitments.

This election is about choosing someone who will stand up for Barrow and Thingoe every day not just at election time. I am committed to being that councillor: accessible, hardworking, and focused on delivering real results for the community.

Together, we can build a fairer, stronger future for Barrow and Thingoe.

Helen has been a resident of Bury St Edmunds for over 20 years has spent her whole working life as a solicitor in Suffolk specialising in Criminal and Family Law.

When she first qualified she could see that the victims of crime were not well served and became involved with Victim Support and groups concerned with Survivors of Domestic Abuse and Sexual Assault as well as the CAB (Citizens Advice)

She is the daughter of a Polish survivor of the Nazi concentration Camps, who came to Britain in 1948 and then married a local girl. Helen shares her father's pride in the values behind our democracy. She is ever conscious that our freedoms and opportunities cannot be taken for granted.

Meeting and working with so many people from different walks of life has led her to believe that there is no such thing as an ordinary person. In politics she believes in experts and that includes all citizens, who so often are well aware of the problems we face but also have the solutions.

Since joining the Liberal Democrats in 2014 she has been an active campaigner and a candidate in both in local and national elections.

If elected in May she would pledge to consult and keep her constituents informed on the political decisions that are being made and to channel the wisdom that exists in our communities in the debates with the majority party.

Mark was born and raised in Beccles, where he attended local schools. A student at Westminster College, Oxford, where he gained a degree in Theology and was elected President of the Student Union. On returning to Suffolk, Mark was elected to Beccles Town Council in 1991, served as Deputy Mayor in the first term and later, as Town Mayor.

He has worked for more than 27 years for the Conservative Party as a Constituency Agent. In 1998, Mark was elected to Waveney District Council and served as a District Councillor for 21 years. He was Leader of the Council for nine years, longer than anyone else in the Council’s history. Mark also served as Chairman of the Council for a year and was part of the project which created the new East Suffolk Council, which was established in April 2019.

In 2005, elected as a Suffolk County Councillor for Beccles. Over the last 15 years, Mark has been Chairman of the Scrutiny Committee and from 2011-2015 was Leader of Suffolk County Council.

In September 2020 was ordained into the Church of England and serves as part of the Ministry Team in Beccles.

He remains passionate about his work as a Councillor and for the Beccles Division, which is where he lives and has his roots.

I am pleased to announce that I am to stand in the upcoming County elections for the people of Stoke Park and Sprites (was Chantry division but new boundaries mean these two wards will come under a new name of Belstead Hills). I lived in this area for 47 years and know and understand the issues.
Let me make a few things clear. 
I have never been afraid of losing. My residents decide and that’s that. No arguments. 
When you are a season ticket holder for over 30 years, for a team like Ipswich you expect the great with the bad moments. It’s part of the excitement and joy. 
Politics is the same. I’m a true Blue!
It requires constant hard work, not soundbites or leaving for another team.
All I ask is that I’m judged on how I have helped hundreds of people and carried out my many responsibilities over 20 years. I have always been honest and outspoken and put residents first. And now as a Cabinet Member I dedicate my whole week to Communities across the town and Suffolk.
There will be elections again next year for the new unitary shadow council, which has now been paused until after May because it’s impossible to do both properly, so this time it’s a short term position and also for LGR to be delivered as residents deserve, the council officers need experienced councillors - This is not the time for Learning on the Job Newbies promising the earth and talking about issues that are not the remit of local authorities, like immigration.
It’s not a time for myths and half stories of populism.
It’s a time for grown up politics whether the government decides 1 unitary or 3 for Suffolk.
I’ll be delivering and knocking and if anyone wants to help even if just once, please let me know.

Robin has lived in Ipswich for all but 2 of his 54 years. He joined the party in 1984 whilst at college and after graduating worked for the party as organiser until the 1987 general election.

From 1992-1994 he was Business Manager for Liberal Democrat News. He has worked as Political Assistant to to the Liberal Democrat Leaders of Chelmsford, Norwich and Isle of Wight and served as agent to Sir Bob Russell when he was MP for Colchester. He has also been a candidate or agent in elections in Ipswich many times.

He is currently a Royal Mail postman, part time researcher and supporting artist. He has been married to Hilary for over 30 years.

Paul grew up just outside Ipswich but has lived and worked in the town for the last thirty years. It is a great place to live, being just an hour from London and having a vibrant waterfront area. Paul was elected as the County Councillor for the Bixley Division in a by-election in May 2016.

Paul first joined the Conservative Party because he believes in helping people achieve their ambitions by creating the environment to allow people to thrive in their personal life and career. Improved health and economic wellbeing go hand in hand.

When it comes to Ipswich, the balancing act between providing more homes for a growing population whilst services can keep up with the growth is a difficult one. However, Paul is committed to supporting improved infrastructure for all forms of transport such as overhauling the Copdock Interchange and making sure the bus and rail terminal in the town centre work in a joined-up way together.

Paul is just as committed to seeing improved services for mental health and wellbeing, something that is even more important now given the experiences of many people during the COVID lockdowns.

I’m Laurence Bradley, your Labour Party candidate for the Bixley division in the Suffolk County Council elections on May 7th.

I’ve been part of this community for over 30 years and live locally. My children grew up here, I spent 15 years as a Scout leader, and I am now a trustee of one of our fantastic local charities, Iceni. Supporting the community has always been a big part of who I am.

As the weather brightens, you’ll often find me cycling along Suffolk’s quiet roads, gardening, or enjoying the cultural events in Ipswich’s parks.

Professionally, I have spent much of my career helping organisations use technology to better serve people, with a particular focus on supporting individuals through change and new ways of working. Local government in Suffolk is about to undergo its biggest transformation in over 50 years, moving from one authority to three unitary councils. I want to help ensure that this transition is as smooth as possible for residents, and I believe my experience puts me in a strong position to represent Bixley through that change.

While social media plays an important role in modern politics, it’s conversations on the doorstep that matter most to me. Hearing directly from residents is an irreplaceable privilege. Please do get in touch and let me know what matters to you.

Being a Green IBC candidate comes naturally to Stephanie, flowing from a life long concern with the growing pressure on our natural world and the consequent issues which the younger community will inherit if we don’t do more to slow this pressure down.   "We can do better and we can all do more! But we do need our representatives in local and national government to help make this happen.    I’ve run my own business in organic whole foods and environmentally friendly products, I’m a member of Spring round allotment field and I believe that strengthening feelings of community can help us achieve a more sustainable future. So vote Green and we can make a better future."

I am standing for election to the County Council as I hope to represent the community in which I have lived all my life, and I believe I can offer experience, energy, and enthusiasm if I have the privilege of doing so.

Andrew and his family have lived in Westleton for the past 14 years.

Andrew has been a Public Affairs Consultant for most of his working life which has included spells living in New York, Brussels and London.

Since moving to Suffolk, where his father's side of his family originated, he has involved himself in local community initiatives including helping draft Westleton's Parish Plan and being a long-standing member of the community speed watch team. Andrew was a leading member of the Lib Dem group submitting an in depth submission to the last consultation on the Sizewell C.

His day job involves working with politicians at the local, National and European level and so he is experienced in the business of working across party lines in the interest of appropriate, proportionate and good decision making.

Andrew became actively involved in party politics after moving to Suffolk. He joined the Liberal Democrats because he was drawn to their open and tolerant approach to society and international relations; their fair and sensible approach to the financing of essential services and their commitment to community and the environment. If elected, Andrew would bring all of these principles to representing the residents of the ward.

[Statement from: https://eastlibdems.org.uk/en/article/2018/1275339/east-suffolk-lib-dems-choose-andrew-turner-as-their-candidate-for-the-wenhaston-westleton-by-election]

Adria Pittock has lived and worked in Ipswich for 25 years. Adria says “We are in a cost-of-living crisis, vulnerable pensioners have had winter fuel payments removed, and now council tax is going up again while Labour cut local services to the bone. We need councillors free to stand up for local residents, not another Labour councillor told what to do by Keir Starmer.“

Kay has lived in Needham Market for twenty-four years and served as a Town Councillor for fifteen years, on three occasions very proudly as Town Mayor. She was elected to the County Council in October 2018 and currently serve on the Scrutiny Committee. As she works locally part-time, Kay has the time to be committed to helping the residents of the area.

Kay is very community minded serving as a Trustee of several charities, one of which is the Needham Market & District Family Carers group, which offers help and support to those caring for loved ones. My involvement with the Theobald’s Endowed Foundation involves awarding financial grants to young adults living in Needham Market and Barking who are embarking on further education.

Kay’s main priorities are:

1. Ensuring the sustainability of local services, which are of paramount importance to the villages in the division and equally supporting the many and varied businesses in Needham Market’s High Street.

2. Work with our partners to ensure that we have access to vital health services as we have an aging population with complex health needs.

3. Improving road safety and the state of rural roads.

4. Fighting for better facilities for our young people.

Kay says that she will serve the residents of the division to the best of her ability and make sure that she is always available to hear ideas and concerns.

OVER THE PAST FOUR YEARS IT HAS BEEN A PRIVILEGE TO FUND THE FOLLOWING GROUPS:

1) FLEDGELINGS PRE SCHOOL 2) GLADE ACADEMY 3) BRECKLAND SCHOOL 4) BRANDON IN BLOOM 5) BRANDON PAROCHIAL CHURCH 6) BEST OF BRANDON 7) BRANDON ALLOTMENTS 8) BRANDON BOWLS CLUB 9) CITIZENS ADVICE 10) BRANDON FOOTBALL CLUB  11) BRANDON MODEL ENGINEERS  12) BRANDON HERITAGE CENTRE

I AM A WEST SUFFOLK INDEPENDENT SEEKING RE-ELECTION AS A DISTRICT COUNCILLOR AND TOWN COUNCILLOR.

IF RE-ELECTED I WILL CONTINUE TO FUND LOCAL COMMUNITY GROUPS.

I AM ALSO COMMITTED TO:

1) THE RETURN OF A FULL BLOOD TESTING FACILITY IN BRANDON 2) AFFORDABLE HOUSING 3) ALL WEATHER PITCH AT BRECKLAND SCHOOL  4) DEVELOPMENT OF THE NEW CEMETERY 5) HGV BAN 6) ONCE AGAIN BACK THE AMAZING BRANDON IN BLOOM

"Tony Blacker is the Labour Party candidate for Stoke Park at the Borough Council election on Thursday 5th May 2022. 

Tony has spent most of his adult life in the ward, having lived on Cambridge Drive and then St Catherines Court until February 2019. He now lives on Prince of Wales Drive. 

He recently lead a campaign to get the lights on the Birkfield Drive underpass fixed after they had been broken for over 8 years, and had become a crime hotspot, and has been working with residents to fix a number of local issues, from broken road signs to issues tenants face with residents associations to problems with Gateway online accounts to repairs to broken pavements. He is proud to be an active member of this community and wants to continue making a positive difference to Stoke Park.

He works full time as a programme lead in the NHS, working to make technology deliver better information to both staff and patients. He enjoys encountering different opinions to his own, and finding ways in which people of opposing outlooks can work together to a common goal.

He believes his history of having been variously self employed, a “gig economy” worker, a social housing tenant, and now a home owner working in the public sector brings with it a different perspective to the role which will allow him to not only connect with residents but also to represent them effectively."

"Adam Robertson has lived in Carlton Colville ward for the past 14 years. educated at The Denes High School. Adam has since graduated from Aberystwyth University prior to his successful MA at UEA. Norwich in 2015.

Hard working, committed local campaigner Adam has been involved with several community projects including, founding the Waveney Lodgers, a disability football team. He is currently employed by a disability charity based near Norwich."

Christopher has been honoured to serve and represent the people of Suffolk as a County Councillor since 2012. He also served as a District Councillor from 2011-2019.

Christopher was educated at the University of Leeds and Queens’ College, Cambridge, and is am a qualified teacher and former Headmaster. Since his election to the County Council, he has held the offices of Cabinet Member, Vice-Chairman, Chairman and Deputy Leader of the Council.

His division of Belstead Brook is a diverse area, comprising Pinewood, Sproughton, Hintlesham, Belstead and Burstal. It has both suburban and rural areas and reflects all the issues dealt with by the Highways, Planning, Consumer Affairs, Adult Social Care, and Education services at Suffolk County Council.

Christopher believes that the greatest honour and duty for any Councillor is to fear nothing, stand up and tell the truth, and represent all the people in your division, whether they voted for you or not.

"Leigh is an IT engineer at BT where he has worked since leaving school. He moved to this part of Suffolk with his wife Caroline when BT offered him a new role at their Ipswich base, then later transferred to BT’s groundbreaking research centre at Martlesham where he is responsible for the day to day running of its computer halls.  At home Leigh is the sound and lighting man for Bildeston’s am dram society and a key fundraiser the annual village music festival.  He and Caroline’s riding days are on hold because their beloved German warmblood mare Paris – stabled in Elmsett - is now too old.  Leigh said: “Having grown up in a city, village life means a lot to me. I’m standing to be a councillor because I care about the villages in this area and it seems to me the current Conservative administration does not.”"

Although born in Lancashire, John moved to East Suffolk before his second birthday. Educated at Southwold, Godalming and London, John's first career as a land surveyor led him to work for 10 years in the Middle-East, before marriage to a girl from the Saints precipitated a change of career to become a secondary school geography teacher in Suffolk for 20 years and then in south Norfolk as a college lecturer. John has lived in The Saints for 40 years.

Retirement has allowed John to spend more time in his environmentally friendly garden, sing in a local Choral Society and tackle a range of renovation projects.

John has been a Churchwarden at St Peter South Elmham for over 20 years and a Liberal Democrat for a similar length of time. He has helped on a practical basis at three Churches in the Saints tidying Churchyards, applying successfully for Grant aid John is also active with Bungay Christian Aid Committee.

From his teaching days, environmental issues have been close to John's heart and he is particularly aware of rural and farming issues. John would want to find out what the electorate wish from their councillors and then act on that within the constraints of the budget. He hopes that among these wishes people would want for a fairer, greener and more caring society.

candidates.p: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party

candidates.stmt:
Originally from Scotland, Elaine has lived in Suffolk with her family for over twenty-five years.

Elaine comes from a farming family and understands what it is to live in a rural community. After running her own successful farm diversification business, Elaine returned to her roots in communications, working in Suffolk County Council as the Sustainable Transport and Tourism Officer for Dedham Vale AONB, and then more widely within the corporate communications team.

Elaine is passionate about all that Suffolk has to offer and understands the challenges facing our rural communities.  Her priorities are to continue to protect and enhance Suffolk’s special environment for generations to come, to work to protect essential services for the more vulnerable in our communities, and to tackle speeding traffic on our rural roads.

Having worked extensively in local government in Suffolk, Elaine knows how to get things done for the benefit of the communities she hopes to represent in Carlford.

In her spare time, Elaine enjoys spending time with her family and dogs discovering more of Suffolk’s countryside.

I'm 23, born and bred in Suffolk, educated, employed and running my own business here. I know the pressures local people face. I'll fight for better maintained roads, real value for your council tax and practical decisions that put Suffolk first. A fresh voice, ready to challenge the status quo.
"Dave O'Neill has been an active local campaigner for many years. Joint president of East Suffolk Liberal Democrats he has championed many local causes in his home town of Bungay."

I’m proud to announce that I’m standing in the County Council elections.

I’ve decided to stand because I’ve seen the Conservatives run our county into the ground by failing to prioritise local investment while focusing on their failed "One Suffolk" plan. They’ve failed to tackle potholes and keep our roads safe, with deaths and serious injuries now at a 10-year high. At the same time, more traffic and heavy lorries are being pushed through our communities, with little investment to protect residents.

They’ve also failed on public transport showing no real ambition to build a reliable bus network, missing out on major funding opportunities, and cutting support for rural routes that people depend on.

There is also a growing threat from Reform, who want to cut vital public services in rural areas and dismiss the urgency of climate action, putting the countryside we cherish in Constable Country at risk.

Here in Babergh, the Green Party has shown a better way. As the largest group on the District Council, Greens have protected and restored nature through the Biodiversity Action Plan, and supported neighbourhood plans so communities can shape development in their area.

I’m standing because I believe we need a council that puts our villages first. Safer streets, better transport, and real action to protect our beautiful countryside in Constable Country.

If elected, I will stand up for our communities and fight for the services we deserve

I am a proud Great Cornard resident, mum of two and small business owner, so I know the challenges businesses face and pressures families are under.

As your councillor I  will fight locally to:
Back local people
Champion small businesses
Fix the roads
Tackle council mismanagement 
Strengthen and protect our local community and ensure the community continues to benefit from them.
Work hard to represent you and ensure your best interests are served with a common sense approach.

Being a resident of the division I will be representing, I am standing up for my own neighbourhood. Being a strong voice for fellow residents.

I’m standing for election as County Councillor to advocate for Great Cornard on Suffolk Council. 
I’m eager to make our local environment, including the roads, cleaner, safer and working better for us all, and to improve Council services.  
I want to help bring the community together to work towards a more hopeful future.

As one of your parish councillors for the past four years, I have seen developments passing through the planning process with little concern for over-development in our village.

We are now at the point where there is almost no spare space left to build! In addition, our infrastructure has not kept up with the amount of new housing built. We are experiencing problems with traffic and flooding due to lack of considering the impact that new housing developments have on the rest of the village. I will resist Great Cornard turning into one big housing development by working to protect our local facilities such as our library, children's centre and essential bus services, and opposing the time and money wasted on ever-changing proposals to improve local roads.

Marjorie and the local Lib Dem team have been conducting surveys of local residents in Sudbury and Great Cornard .

In Great Cornard, you told us that you are concerned about losing local services and open spaces and want to see improvements in local transport links. There is a lot of concern in the village about new housing developments and that any new housing should prioritise housing for retirement

The natural beauty of the countryside on our doorstep is one of the features that attracts people to come and live here in Great Cornard, but proposed developments threaten our views of the landscape, ecology and open spaces we use for work, leisure and wellbeing. Marjorie will prioritise action to reduce air pollution and flooding by pushing for better maintenance of the infrastructure of the built-on areas of the village. She will also promote improvements to our public rights of way to increase safety and access for everyone


About Marjorie

Elected as Great Cornard Parish Councillor in 2017
Living in Great Cornard with her family for over 15 years with children attending schools in Great Cornard
Currently working as a Environmental Auditor in the waste and recycling industry
Actively involved i

Ever since I was a teenager I have been convinced that the way politicians are running the world – driving for infinte economic growth above quality of life – is not achieving happiness and is wrecking the planet. I am a former national newspaper journalist but since 2010 I have lived in South Suffolk with my wife and two boys running a smallholding and a PR and media consultancy. I was Babergh District Council's first elected Green councillor 2014-2015 and in 2016 I was elected as the first Green county councillor in south Suffolk. I was re-elected with a much bigger majority in 2020.
South Suffolk needs someone to defend our market towns and villages from the wave of suburban over-development that is about to wash over them thanks to a supine local authority and the most aggressive pro-developer legislation the country has ever seen.
If elected I would put bus and train investment before new road building; independent businesses, the local economy and local communities before multi-nationals, and properly insulated, affordable homes above developer profits.

Hello I am Rupert Perry. I moved to Monks Eleigh 8 years ago from London, where I served as a Labour Councillor in Islington for 25 years.  During that time I learnt how we can be effective in improving the lives of the people we represent. The last thirteen years have been tough for many, and when I arrived here I soon realised that there was much in common between the inner city and beautiful area we live in. Housing, especially social housing is key, but decent services are vital. Bringing some of these in house should save the Council money and improve on what we have. I have experience from my time as a Councillor including conservation, planning, environment, and waste management, and have an understanding about how Local Government works and how to get things done. The Tories have been coasting for too long and we need a shake up. I would be honoured to represent Box Vale and together we would indicate to the Tories that enough is enough. Vote for me to change things for the better All the best Rupert Perry

ipperry70@gmail.com 07484713354

I raised my children in Suffolk, dedicated my career to tackling addiction including county lines, as we see their emergence in this constituency. I worked to improve the safety of communities in Suffolk for over 15 years and helped those fleeing domestic abuse. I believe we have all been let down by self-serving vested political interests, evidenced by our county’s decline. I have the determination and skills needed to put Suffolk people first, represent our community, champion Suffolk for a positive future, stop wasting money and get Suffolk out of the red zone for pot holes!
Suffolk needs a council that will stand up for it, not kow-tow to either Labour or the Tories. We need a change to our funding formula to reflect the changing nature of Suffolk. We need our taxes focussed on frontline spend and not wasted.

A dedicated local campaigner, Andy has lived in Moreton Hall for the last 4 years with his wife and 3 kids.

He truly understands about the challenges Moreton Hall and Eastgate face - from the pressures on our GP surgeries and pharmacies, to cost-of living pressures for residents and businesses, to the range of problems on our roads.

With the changes to the boundaries bringing Rushbrooke with Rougham into this ward for the first time, Andy is also committed to ensuring that the voices and priorities for these villages are not forgotten and are heard just as much as those in the rest of the ward.

Andy will be a strong voice fighting for sustainable funding for our local health
services to balance new houses and our fair share of funding to fix the problems on
our roads - from potholes, to issues with parking and HGVs, to better bus services. Andy will keep fighting for a solution to the Royal Mail parking saga and wants to see improved accountability when it comes to potholes and pavement defects - why are some potholes investigated in two days, whilst multiple ones in Rougham go over a month without seemingly even being investigated?

As the Vice-Chair of the Moreton Hall and Eastgate Residents Association, he has
worked with fellow committee members, residents, councils, schools, police and businesses to tackle many issues: such as:
- Parking around the Royal Mail depot
- Tackling anti-social behaviour
- Road safety around our primary schools.
- Proposals to make Barton Road one-way only as part of active travel plans
- A lack of investment in local health services in planning applications for new homes
 
Andy’s priority is to continue to be a visible, vocal champion for you. From councillor
clinics, to visiting community groups and businesses, to regular updates – he does not want people having to ask “Who is our councillor?”

**Quote from Andy**
"I'm proud to be a party of this community. We have shown that when residents, businesses and councillors of all poli

My name is Jon London and I am the Liberal Democrat District Councillor for Exning. I look forward to being County Councillor for Exning and Newmarket. I’ve lived in West Suffolk for most of my life, moving to the Newmarket area in 2015. My wife and I fell in love with Newmarket and started our family here. I care deeply about our village and Town, it’s the only home my two children have ever known.

To me this community is many things. It’s chatting to other parents while walking to Exning Primary School, it’s kicking a football about on the Rec, it’s chatting to friends and neighbours as they walk their dogs, long summer cycle rides down North End Road and exploring the countryside. It’s the lantern festival down the Yellow Brick Road and shows at the Kings theatre.

Before going into politics I worked in science communication, including doing workshops in schools and working on TV shows such as Brainiac.

Seamus was born and schooled in Felixstowe and after a career in education and international development moved back to the UK with his family in 2008, choosing to settle back in his hometown where his dad had been a GP for nearly 40 years.

"Frustration has brought me here. As a First Responder, I see first-hand the effects of government cuts on people when they need help most."
"Every day and night, I see NHS workers battling with incredible determination and skill to save people’s lives, when the resources they should have at their disposal have been cut below survival level."

He added: "Sizewell C is a dangerous and unnecessary project.

Passionate about clean air and his surroundings, Eamonn vehemently opposes fracking, and is actively involved in the campaign against Sizewell C. He and his wife Catherine have seven children and their dog Luna.

Dear Woodbridge Neighbour, 
I have had the privilege of serving as your County Councillor for the past two and a half years, and it has been an honour to work on behalf of Woodbridge. During that time, I have focused on delivering practical improvements that make a real difference—from resurfacing the riverside pathway to tackling flooding on the A12, improving pavements in the Thoroughfare, and securing better bus services for residents and students.

I believe in not accepting “no” when there is a better outcome to be achieved. That determination is what drives me to keep pushing for results for our community.

Looking ahead, Woodbridge faces important challenges. We need strong, experienced representation to guide us through Local Government Reorganisation, to stand up for our town as major projects like Sizewell C place increasing pressure on our infrastructure, and to ensure schemes such as Active Travel and A12 improvements work in our best interests.

I will continue to be a clear voice for Woodbridge—championing safer crossings, better access, improved local facilities, and the protection of our environment.

I am committed to working hard, listening to residents, and delivering for our community.

Thank you
